clauses.clause: A command starts at beginning of line and newline ends command, except special cases.
clauses.notes: Parser treats NEWLINE and comma-soft-break as statement delimiters.

clauses.clause: Comma acts as virtual newline/soft-command-break.
clauses.notes: Lexer emits NEWLINE tokens for commas.

clauses.clause: Line continuation with ... and unicode ellipsis U+2026.
clauses.notes: Both ASCII and unicode ellipsis are accepted at line end.

clauses.clause: A line with continuation may not be followed by an empty line (except continuation-only line behavior).
clauses.notes: Lexer rejects continuation-followed empty lines except continuation-only line handling per spec text.

clauses.clause: Single-line comments begin with BTW and terminate at newline.
clauses.notes: BTW after statements and comma-separated statements is supported; marker matching is strict-case (`BTW` only).

clauses.clause: Block comments begin with OBTW and end with TLDR.
clauses.notes: Unterminated block comments produce lexical errors; block comment markers are strict-case (`OBTW`/`TLDR` only); comma-inline OBTW/TLDR handoff is supported per examples, while `TLDR` with trailing statement text requires comma/newline boundary.

clauses.clause: Formatting controls (comments, comma soft-breaks, continuation) require a deterministic preprocessing order.
clauses.notes: Adjudication: lexer performs newline normalization/comment handling/continuation/comma conversion with string shielding, then parser applies phrase rewrites; ordering-sensitive regressions include inline TLDR comma handoff and continuation shielding cases.

clauses.clause: Multiword and punctuated keyword forms are parsed by exact phrase tokens (e.g., O RLY?, WTF?, IM OUTTA YR).
clauses.notes: Adjudication: punctuated keywords require canonical token shape (`RLY?`, `WTF?`), phrase collapse for IM IN/IM OUTTA applies only to same-line token pairs, and keyword recognition is strict-case for direct keyword tokens.

clauses.clause: Programs start with HAI and end with KTHXBYE.
clauses.notes: Implementation enforces explicit closing KTHXBYE and strict post-close boundary: no same-line or trailing post-close material is accepted except whitespace.

clauses.clause: Spec does not standardize version-number handling by implementations.
clauses.notes: Implementation intentionally enforces strict HAI 1.3 as project policy within unspecified spec space, with numeric header-token requirement for version position.

clauses.clause: Scope text says local main/function scope and no global scope.
clauses.notes: Adjudication: general execution uses lexical frames (main/function locals). In object-function context, bare IT resolves in method activation-local ordinary scope (never receiver-slot/global alias).

clauses.clause: I HAS A <variable> [ITZ <value>] declaration.
clauses.notes: Declaration + initializer semantics implemented with boxed lexical bindings.

clauses.clause: Literal initializers instantiate the corresponding primitive type; identifier/expression initializers use the resulting value.
clauses.notes: Declaration initializer values are used directly; literal forms produce their corresponding value domain (NUMBR/NUMBAR/TROOF/YARN/NOOB).

clauses.clause: Identifiers must start with a letter, followed by letters/digits/underscores only.
clauses.notes: Identifier domain adjudication is ASCII-leading-letter + ASCII letter/digit/underscore tail (`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$`): mixed-case/underscore forms are accepted, while non-ASCII direct identifiers and malformed forms (_x, x-y, ++, etc.) are rejected at parser/runtime identifier gates.

clauses.clause: Identifier shape text overlaps with keyword token forms (e.g., AN, MKAY).
clauses.notes: Adjudication: strict parser reserves keyword token lexemes in direct identifier positions to avoid grammar ambiguity with keyword-delimited forms. Ambiguity examples: `VISIBLE SMOOSH x AN y MKAY` (AN separator vs identifier argument) and `VISIBLE SMOOSH "A" MKAY` (explicit closer vs identifier with implicit EOL closure). Keyword-shaped names remain available through `SRS` where 1.3 allows dynamic identifiers.

clauses.clause: I HAS A <variable> ITZ A <type> default values.
clauses.notes: ITZ A declaration defaults are restricted to TROOF/YARN/NUMBR/NUMBAR/NOOB/BUKKIT. Prototyping remains disambiguated as ITZ LIEK A <parent> (plain) and ITZ A <parent> SMOOSH ... (mixin); bare ITZ A <non-built-in> declarations are rejected. Bare ITZ TYPE-word remains a legal TYPE-literal expression initializer.

clauses.clause: I HAS A <variable> initializes to NOOB.
clauses.notes: Uninitialized declarations bind to NOOB.

clauses.clause: Function types behave like variables and share the same namespace.
clauses.notes: Same-scope redeclaration now errors across variable/function bindings; assignment may still overwrite an existing function binding as shown by spec example.

clauses.clause: SRS may be used anywhere a regular identifier is expected.
clauses.notes: Supported across declaration/assignment targets, slot access, function/method/object names, loop labels/updaters, method-call names, and object name/parent positions. Parameter binders remain direct-only (`YR <ident-token>`), and dynamic `SRS` parameter names are parse-rejected.

clauses.clause: A becomes optional in declaration when using SRS target.
clauses.notes: Variable declaration accepts omitted article in strict 1.3 (e.g., I HAS ..., I HAS SRS ...); if an article is present there, it must be A (AN rejected), while other forms keep explicit-article requirements per grammar text.

clauses.clause: <variable> R <expression> assignment.
clauses.notes: Assignments require lvalue targets (identifier/SRS target or slot chain), mutate existing bindings only, and raise runtime error for undeclared identifier assignment.

clauses.clause: Assignment statements are of form <variable> <assignment operator> <expression>.
clauses.notes: Parser enforces variable/lvalue target shape for both R assignment and IS NOW A cast-assignment statements; non-variable LHS expressions are rejected at parse time.

clauses.clause: Primitive-type section contains legacy 'BUKKIT reserved for future expansion' wording.
clauses.notes: Adjudication: treat later dedicated BUKKIT section (lines 612+) as authoritative 1.3 behavior; legacy wording in type prose is informationally stale.

clauses.clause: <variable> R NOOB drops that variable reference; reclamation is implementation-managed.
clauses.notes: Runtime assignment sets binding to NOOB. GC timing is delegated to host runtime; semantic tests validate reference/value behavior only.

clauses.clause: NUMBR/NUMBAR lexical form requires contiguous numeric tokens.
clauses.notes: Malformed literals like 1..23, 2., and '- 123' are rejected.

clauses.clause: Casting YARN to numeric types errors on non-numeric forms.
clauses.notes: Runtime enforces strict decimal numeric lexical forms for YARN->number coercion; NUMBAR->NUMBR truncates toward zero.

clauses.clause: NOOB cannot be used in typed operations like numeric math; such use errors.
clauses.notes: Arithmetic coercion rejects NOOB in numeric contexts rather than treating it as zero.

clauses.clause: Casting/printing NUMBAR as YARN defaults to two decimal places.
clauses.notes: NUMBAR->YARN printing truncates to a default maximum of two decimal places (no forced zero-padding); because 1.3 defines no precision-control surface, default precision is treated as the only mode.

clauses.clause: Primitive values are immutable; built-ins return new values.
clauses.notes: Arithmetic and string concatenation do not mutate source bindings; reassignment is explicit via R/IS NOW A. The singleton part of the clause is semantically sharp only for WIN/FAIL/NOOB; numeric/string representation identity remains implementation-level and non-observable under strict equality semantics.

clauses.clause: Binary operator syntax uses optional [AN] separator.
clauses.notes: Grammar supports both canonical and omitted-AN forms across full binary operator family; non-spec separator token AND is rejected.

clauses.clause: Variadics use MKAY terminator with optional AN separators between args.
clauses.notes: Parser accepts explicit MKAY and line-terminated closure for open variadics (ALL OF/ANY OF), but rejects implicit omission before continued same-statement tails (for example before `!` or `AN YR`). Runtime policy pins left-to-right evaluation with short-circuit behavior for ALL OF/ANY OF (stop at first decisive operand).

clauses.clause: Operators are spelled as listed in spec (e.g., DIFF OF, QUOSHUNT OF).
clauses.notes: Non-spec aliases are not tokenized as operator keywords.

clauses.clause: Math uses integer mode for NUMBR/NUMBR and floating mode when either operand is NUMBAR.
clauses.notes: QUOSHUNT and other math operators use integer arithmetic when both coerced operands are exact integers; if either operand is NUMBAR/inexact, floating arithmetic is used and printed with NUMBAR YARN formatting rules.

clauses.clause: Equality compares numerically for NUMBR/NUMBAR operands; otherwise no implicit cast (e.g. "3" vs 3 is FAIL).
clauses.notes: BOTH SAEM/DIFFRINT use spec-aware equality: numeric mode only when both operands are numeric; non-numeric forms compare without implicit coercion and preserve host identity behavior for object/function values.

clauses.clause: SMOOSH concatenation supports optional AN and optional MKAY closure by line ending.
clauses.notes: SMOOSH forms with and without AN/MKAY are exercised in runtime tests. Strict arity policy is one-or-more arguments (zero-arg parse rejection; one-arg identity accepted), with eager left-to-right operand evaluation preserving side effects from earlier operands when later operands error.

clauses.clause: MAEK expression cast operator.
clauses.notes: MAEK compiles through cast-value with explicit conversion semantics; optional [A] token is supported only for MAEK forms; cast target designators are restricted to TROOF/YARN/NUMBR/NUMBAR/NOOB with strict-case spelling; MAEK is non-mutating and leaves source bindings/slots unchanged.

clauses.clause: Casting assignment statement <variable> IS NOW A <type>.
clauses.notes: IS NOW A updates only the assignment target; cast target designators are restricted to TROOF/YARN/NUMBR/NUMBAR/NOOB with strict-case spelling.

clauses.clause: VISIBLE prints concatenated argument YARNs; ! suppresses newline.
clauses.notes: VISIBLE supports adjacency-separated multi-arg output and newline suppression, including continued-line argument lists and comma soft-break command boundaries; VISIBLE does not mutate IT.

clauses.clause: GIMMEH reads input into variable.
clauses.notes: EOF maps to NOOB in current runtime behavior; target must already be a declared variable designator and undeclared targets raise runtime errors.

clauses.clause: Bare expression statements update IT; IT remains local until replaced by the next bare expression.
clauses.notes: Main/function execution uses lexical IT bindings; function call frames initialize local IT independently of caller scope.

clauses.clause: IT mutation points are constrained to explicit runtime-updating forms.
clauses.notes: Closed-list adjudication: IT updates only on bare expression statements; assignment-family statements and other statement wrappers do not mutate IT by themselves.

clauses.clause: O RLY? branches on implicit IT value.
clauses.notes: O RLY? consumes implicit IT only; parser lowers if-stmt condition to IT identifier.

clauses.clause: YA RLY branch runs when IT is truthy; otherwise NO WAI branch runs when present.
clauses.notes: Runtime branch selection is driven by current IT truthiness; assignment statements do not rewrite IT prior to branch selection.

clauses.clause: WTF? uses literal OMG cases; literals must be unique; GTFO exits switch.
clauses.notes: Duplicate literals are rejected, parser rejects interpolated YARN OMG literals (:{var}), and OMGWTF executes only when no OMG literal matched (matched-case fallthrough does not trigger OMGWTF). Numeric pairs use numeric-mode equality in duplicate detection and runtime matching (`1` and `1.0` are duplicate/equal), while mixed numeric/non-numeric pairs remain non-coercing.

clauses.clause: IM IN YR/IM OUTTA YR loops with updater and TIL/WILE conditions.
clauses.notes: Loop-local update variable scope and GTFO behavior are tested; updater/condition matrix tests pin pre-body condition checks with post-body updater execution across UPPIN/NERFIN and TIL/WILE forms.

clauses.clause: HOW IZ I definition + I IZ call syntax.
clauses.notes: Function declaration, invocation, and argument passing are covered. Definition-site opener accepts both `HOW IZ` and `HOW DUZ` spellings; call syntax remains `I IZ` / `<receiver> IZ` (no `DUZ` call operator).

clauses.clause: FOUND YR returns value; GTFO returns NOOB from function context.
clauses.notes: Control semantics implemented via host escapes in execution context.

clauses.clause: BUKKIT declaration and slot-oriented object model.
clauses.notes: Object model includes parent-chain slot lookup, copy-on-write slot assignment, and special slots parent/omgwtf/izmakin.

clauses.clause: Slot creation/assignment form uses <object> HAS A <slotname> ITZ <expression>.
clauses.notes: Canonical explicit form remains `HAS A <slot> ITZ <expression>`. Adjudication accepts `<object> HAS A <slot>` as shorthand initializing to NOOB (including but not limited to `ME`). Bare HAS/`HAS AN` remain rejected.

clauses.clause: Alternate object syntax O HAI IM <object> [IM LIEK <parent>] KTHX.
clauses.notes: Object-body method definitions and inheritance clone semantics are covered.

clauses.clause: Slot access operator forms: prose '-' plus displayed 'Z and SRS dynamic slot names.
clauses.notes: Static and dynamic slot access/mutation forms are covered; adjudicated strict policy accepts both '-' and ''Z spellings as equivalent slot access syntax in order to reconcile line-729 prose with line-732/738 displayed forms. Dynamic `SRS` slot keys are typed (`NUMBR`/`YARN`) and do not coerce (e.g., `0` and `"0"` remain distinct keys).

clauses.clause: Slot-access function calls resolve function-variable lookup from the receiver object namespace.
clauses.notes: Slot-callable functions are invoked with receiver-projected scope, matching the funkin/prefix behavior (spec lines 804/810).

clauses.clause: Mixin inheritance syntax using SMOOSH in object construction.
clauses.notes: Parser and runtime support IM LIEK/ITZ A mixin forms with reverse-order slot copy semantics.

clauses.clause: Spaces delimit tokens in source text.
clauses.notes: Lexer tokenization relies on whitespace boundaries plus explicit multi-word phrase collapsing.

clauses.clause: Newlines may be CR, LF, or CRLF depending on host.
clauses.notes: Scanner normalizes CR/LF variants into NEWLINE tokens.

clauses.clause: Line continuation markers may chain across multiple lines.
clauses.notes: Continuation chaining is handled by lexer-side line skipping logic.

clauses.clause: A standalone continuation line may include an empty line in the continued command.
clauses.notes: Continuation-only line behavior is accepted while preserving empty-line validation rules.

clauses.clause: BTW comments terminate at newline and ignore continuation markers/comma breaks that follow.
clauses.notes: Lexer consumes remainder of comment line and resumes from next physical line.

clauses.clause: Line continuation and soft-command-break markers are ignored inside YARN literals; unterminated YARN errors.
clauses.notes: String scanner treats raw contents literally except escape expansion and reports unterminated literals.

clauses.clause: KTHXBYE closes the HAI program block.
clauses.notes: Programs without closing KTHXBYE are rejected at parse time; after KTHXBYE only trailing whitespace is accepted (no same-line BTW tails and no trailing comment/code lines).

clauses.clause: YARN escapes include bracketed verbose forms (codepoints and normative names).
clauses.notes: Lexer handles :(hex) and :[UNICODE NAME] with strict validation; normative-name escapes require uppercase letter spelling and are resolved against the spec-cited Unicode 4.1 normative name set (newer names are rejected under strict 1.3).

clauses.clause: :{var} interpolates a variable value as YARN.
clauses.notes: Runtime expands placeholders against current environment; placeholders are identifier-only (no whitespace/expression trimming), and empty/invalid placeholders are rejected.

clauses.clause: TYPE values are bare words like NUMBR/NUMBAR/YARN/TROOF/NOOB/TYPE and may cast to TROOF/YARN.
clauses.notes: Parser maps strict-case TYPE-domain bare words to literal TYPE values in expression position; lowercase lookalikes remain ordinary identifiers; runtime tests cover domain cast behavior to YARN/TROOF.

clauses.clause: Operator arguments may themselves be nested expressions.
clauses.notes: Parser and evaluator support recursive prefix-expression composition.

clauses.clause: Math operators error when arguments cannot be safely cast to numeric values.
clauses.notes: Numeric coercion rejects invalid YARN and NOOB cases in arithmetic contexts.

clauses.clause: Operators with type-specific inputs perform implicit casts and error on unsafe cast.
clauses.notes: Runtime coercion behavior for arithmetic/logical operators follows strict erroring rules.

clauses.clause: Variables may be explicitly recast via assignment-form cast.
clauses.notes: IS NOW A cast assignment is supported for variables and slot lvalues.

clauses.clause: Assignment expressions may refer to the variable being assigned.
clauses.notes: Assignments evaluate RHS against current bindings before writeback, supporting self-reference updates.

clauses.clause: MEBBE branches are checked in order between YA RLY and NO WAI.
clauses.notes: Conditional branch evaluation short-circuits on first truthy MEBBE condition (TROOF-cast semantics); YA RLY is required, MEBBE blocks must appear before NO WAI, and NO WAI remains optional.

clauses.clause: Loop updater supports UPPIN/NERFIN or unary function updater over loop-local variable.
clauses.notes: Loop update supports both +/-1 and function-call updater forms on a temporary loop-local shadow of an already-declared updater variable; undeclared updater names are runtime errors.

clauses.clause: Call arguments are expressions evaluated before function invocation; arity follows definition.
clauses.notes: Runtime evaluates arg procs first and enforces fixed-arity checks at call entry for both namespace calls (`I IZ`) and bukkit slot-method calls (`<object> IZ ...`); side-effect regressions pin left-to-right argument evaluation before body entry.

clauses.clause: The I marker distinguishes namespace function call syntax from bukkit slot-access call syntax.
clauses.notes: I is parsed as call-form syntax (I IZ ...), not as an implicit runtime parameter.

clauses.clause: ME identifies the calling object within method/function object context.
clauses.notes: Method call frames bind ME to receiver and support ME'Z slot access/update.

clauses.clause: Inside object function context, IT lookup is from global namespace.
clauses.notes: Adjudicated under C1/C2/C3 contradiction policy: method IT is activation-local and bypasses receiver-slot lookup. "global namespace" is interpreted as non-receiver ordinary scope, not a process-global IT alias.

clauses.clause: omgwtf special slot handles failed slot lookup with throwing default behavior.
clauses.notes: Missing-slot access invokes effective omgwtf hook; default behavior raises unknown-slot error, custom overrides are supported, synthesized values are memoized to the missing slot, and same-slot re-entry during omgwtf resolution raises deterministic runtime error. Adjudicated policy: omgwtf is invoked with zero args because the spec does not define hook parameters; adding implicit missing-slot-name parameters would require extra unstated machinery.

clauses.clause: izmakin hook method runs after a bukkit is prototyped and before prototype call returns.
clauses.notes: Prototype creation invokes effective izmakin slot when callable after prototype build and before constructor return, including mixin-provided overrides; ordering tests pin that izmakin observes post-mixin, parent-restored state, and reentrant prototype creation runs izmakin per prototype instance.

clauses.clause: Inherited objects expose a special parent slot linked to their prototype parent.
clauses.notes: Prototype creation preserves parent slot semantics, supports reparenting via parent slot mutation, retains cycle-safe lookup traversal, restores declared IM LIEK parent after mixin copy, and treats plain BUKKIT default parent as NOOB.

clauses.clause: Inherited slot assignment declares locally on write when original exists up parent chain (copy-on-write).
clauses.notes: Assignment path writes own slot when inheriting value, preserving parent value while updating child; missing-name assignment raises declaration error.

clauses.clause: Mixin slots are copied in reverse declaration order.
clauses.notes: Mixin application reverses declaration list so earlier mixins win on duplicates, with static snapshot copy semantics after construction and donor effective-visible source-set (including inherited-visible slots/methods); mutable BUKKIT slot values are copied by reference (call-by-sharing).
